methods.description: Process for isolating and identifying benthic diatom strains from sediment cores in the Baltic Sea, including morphological and molecular genetic identification.

steps.description: Benthic diatom strains were isolated from undisturbed sediment cores sampled in nearshore sandy habitats of the Baltic Sea.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate diatom strains from sediment cores

steps.description: Eight diatom species were isolated according to the methodological approach of Stachura-Suchoples et al. (2016).
steps.searchable_summary: apply Stachura-Suchoples method for diatom isolation

steps.description: Strains were morphologically identified using taxonomic literature and compared to the Algae Base 2 database for recent nomenclature.
steps.searchable_summary: morphologically identify diatom strains using literature and database

steps.description: N. phyllepta was molecular-genetically identified using the V4 region of the SSU rRNA, while the rbcL gene was used for other species following Abarca et al. (2014).
steps.searchable_summary: molecular-genetic identification using SSU rRNA and rbcL gene

steps.description: All sequences were submitted to the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) under specific accession numbers.

methods.description: Procedure for maintaining diatom isolates as unialgal stock cultures in enriched medium under controlled conditions.

steps.description: The diatom isolates were maintained as unialgal, but not axenic stock cultures in Guillard's f/2 medium enriched with metasilicate.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain diatom isolates in enriched medium

steps.description: All media were set up with Baltic Sea water enriched with additional sea salt to achieve a final salinity of 15 S A.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare culture media with Baltic Sea water and sea salt
outputs: culture media with 15 S A salinity

steps.description: Cultures were kept at 20°C at a photon fluence density of 30-50 µmol photons m−2 s−1 under a 16:8 h light:dark cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain cultures under controlled temperature and light conditions
